summary: Gruber collects the near-identical congratulatory statements posted by Jeff Bezos, Mark Zuckerberg, Sundar Pichai, Satya Nadella, and Tim Cook after Trump's 2024 election victory. He lets the hollow corporate boilerplate speak for itself before closing with a blistering commentary on the lack of dignity in these performances. He singles out Cook's exclamation mark and the technically incorrect use of 'President Trump' for someone not yet inaugurated. The post is a short, furious indictment of Big Tech leaders falling over themselves to genuflect to power.
key_points: All five major tech CEOs posted nearly interchangeable congratulatory messages within hours of Trump's victory, revealing the performative nature of corporate political engagement.
Gruber notes the protocol error of addressing Trump as 'President Trump' before inauguration, suggesting the CEOs were too eager to ingratiate themselves to observe basic norms.
Cook's exclamation mark is singled out as a particular tell — a small typographic choice that reveals an eagerness Gruber finds distasteful.
The post's structure — letting the quotes accumulate with minimal commentary — is itself the argument: the sameness and sycophancy need no editorializing.
money_quotes: I wonder how much Cook dithered over that cheerful-looking exclamation mark.
I wonder whether the latter four knowingly made the error of addressing former president and president-elect Trump as 'President Trump'.
Our nation only has one president at a time, and that president remains Joe Biden.
I wonder how much it stings to be reminded that all the money in the world cannot buy dignity.
I wonder too, what taste Cheetos-dusted 78-year-old testicles leave in one's mouth.
Whatever the flavor, I hope it lingers.
key_insight: The interchangeable sycophancy of tech's most powerful CEOs toward a returning Trump reveals that immense wealth and corporate power offer no protection against — and may even accelerate — the loss of dignity.
